title: Harbor Chili
description: A hearty, budget-friendly beef and bean chili built for busy family nights.
steps: Dice the onion and drain and rinse the kidney beans.
Brown the ground beef and onion in a large pot over medium heat, then drain excess grease.
Stir in the beans, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, chili powder, and cumin.
Bring to a gentle boil, reduce the heat, and simmer uncovered for 25 to 30 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning, then serve with shredded cheddar if desired.

title: Cheesy Breakfast Quesadillas
description: Crisp tortillas filled with fluffy eggs, cheese, and optional breakfast sausage.
steps: Cook and crumble the sausage in a skillet if using, then transfer it to a plate.
Whisk the eggs and scramble them gently in the same skillet with half the butter.
Lay out the tortillas and divide the eggs, sausage, and cheese over one half of each tortilla.
Fold each tortilla closed and cook with the remaining butter until golden on both sides.
Slice into wedges and serve warm with salsa.

title: Chicken Ranch Wraps
description: Fast chicken wraps with crisp lettuce, cheddar, and creamy ranch for an easy lunch.
steps: Chop the cooked chicken, lettuce, and tomato into bite-size pieces.
Warm the tortillas briefly so they fold without tearing.
Divide the chicken, lettuce, tomato, and cheddar between the tortillas.
Drizzle each wrap with ranch dressing.
Fold in the sides, roll tightly, and cut in half before serving.

title: Fudgy Budget Brownies
description: Simple cocoa brownies with chewy edges and a rich chocolate center.
steps: Heat the oven to 350°F and grease an 8-inch square baking pan.
Melt the butter and stir in the sugar until glossy.
Whisk in the eggs and vanilla.
Fold in the flour, cocoa powder, and salt just until no dry streaks remain.
Fold in chocolate chips if using, spread the batter in the pan, and bake for 24 to 28 minutes.
Cool before slicing so the center can set.

title: Cinnamon Sugar Dessert Nachos
description: Crispy cinnamon tortilla chips with chocolate drizzle for a fast late-night sweet snack.
steps: Heat the oven to 375°F and line a baking sheet.
Cut each tortilla into wedges and brush both sides lightly with melted butter.
Mix the sugar and cinnamon, then sprinkle it evenly over the tortilla wedges.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until crisp and lightly golden.
Melt the chocolate chips in short microwave bursts and drizzle over the warm chips.
Serve immediately with whipped cream if desired.
